75007.subject: Saylor Stress Signal: Crash or Contrarian Buy?
75007.body:
Adrian's DeFi Insights 

Автор: Adrian Pape


Автор: Adrian Pape

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/saylor-stress-signal-crash-contrarian-buy-adrian-pape-o7wrf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BNUXCuvChSnKbilzClyZRGw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0iWSG9CXNqgYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqs12joc~3s-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqs12joc-3s
  
Hey, it's Adrian, Leveraged ETF money doubled in two months. Stocks haven’t been this expensive versus bonds in 95% of the last 50 years. And market liquidity just went negative for the first time since 2021. Three data points, one reading: Late. That third one is the one I watch closest. Excess liquidity. Take money-supply growth, strip out inflation and real economic growth, and whatever’s left is the surplus that has to go somewhere. For years it went into risk assets. That’s the tailwind that supported the stock rally for the last years. It just turned negative. The leading version of it points down for the next three to six months, and it tracks the S&P 500 closely enough that I want to be positioned more risk off for now. The rate machine is tightening without anyone touching a button. The new Fed chair came in more hawkish than expected: Nine of eighteen members now see a hike this year, and futures are pricing two for 2026. The hike isn’t even the point. The gap between the neutral rate and the terminal rate is closing, so policy gets more restrictive just because the market prices it in. No actual FED rate hike is required. Two things are colliding right now. First, stocks have rarely been this expensive relative to bonds. By one read of the last 50 years, the gap is more stretched than 95% of the time, with only 5% of history more extreme. Part of that is earned: corporate profits are strong, so equities keep grinding higher. The other half is bonds getting punished, because inflation is climbing again. U.S. CPI hit 4.2% in May, the fastest pace since 2021, and energy did most of the damage: over 60% of the monthly jump, with the Iran shock pushing gasoline up around 40%. Second, the crowd is leaning into it with leverage. Assets in leveraged equity ETFs doubled in two months, from $39B to $84B, almost all of it chasing the AI trade. ETFs have pulled a record $837B in the first five months of the year alone. You don’t need me to call the top. Look at where we are: Expensive, crowded, levered, and late. Decide for yourself which inning of the bubble that is, and how much longer it runs. Now I don’t want to be a perma bear and this is not a crash signal. The link between liquidity and price is loose, not mechanical. First hikes came before perfectly good years for stocks in 1997, 1999, 2004 and 2015. Only 2022 burned everyone.

 Гороскоп личности -- ваш
путеводитель по жизни 🌌
Добрый день, Олег! Иногда человек приходит в астрологию не за прогнозом, а за простым ответом: почему я снова выбираю одно и то же, хотя хочу по-другому? Почему одни и те же ситуации повторяются в отношениях, в работе, в деньгах, в самооценке? И почему советов вокруг много, а ясности -- нет.
В такие моменты полезно не «искать правильный ответ снаружи», а увидеть, как вы устроены изнутри. Именно для этого существует Гороскоп личности -- подробная расшифровка вашей натальной карты, где собраны ваши сильные стороны, уязвимости, привычные сценарии и точки роста. Короткая версия доступна бесплатно.
 PERSONAL.LICHNY_KABINET|%Открыть Гороскоп личности [ https://birthchart.astromix.net/ru/prod/horoscope/masterv2?user=0dC8ClzHWrfrWti6LJJA_opLoca-HNNamA4NbU ]
В интернете чаще всего ищут одно и то же, только разными словами.
«В чем мое предназначение?» -- на самом деле про страх ошибиться и потратить годы не туда.
«Почему мне сложно удерживать стабильность?» -- часто про внутренний конфликт между безопасностью и свободой.
«Почему я так реагирую на близких?» -- про личные границы и потребность в принятии.
«Почему я не могу решиться?» -- про механизм принятия решений, который заложен в карте и работает независимо от силы воли.
Без мистики и без морали. Есть вещи, которые обычно не хотят знать о себе -- но именно они экономят годы. Например:
почему вы притягиваете людей одного типа, даже когда обещаете себе «больше так не выбирать»;
где вы склонны переоценивать себя, а где -- наоборот занижать планку;
что именно у вас запускает тревогу, раздражение или желание «все бросить»;
как вы на самом деле строите близость и почему иногда вас не слышат, даже если вы говорите правильно;
какие внутренние установки мешают расти в д

75005.subject: The best film and television of 2026 (so far)
75005.body:
The Economist’s week ahead

Автор: The Economist


Автор: The Economist

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/best-film-television-2026-so-far-the-economist-qkwme?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BCz2oEs8aTnC6HKpX3s9DgQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=21BnbtOkgQgYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqrz9r1c~1y-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqrz9r1c-1y
  
Sign up for free here to receive this newsletter in your inbox every Saturday. You’ll also get our weekly newsletter with an exclusive introduction from our editor-in-chief. A few weeks ago I had an unexpected experience at the cinema. I went into “The Sheep Detectives” expecting it to be a lighthearted, silly yarn: Hugh Jackman, after all, was playing shepherd to a flock of computer-generated sheep. Yet Craig Mazin’s script was anything but woolly. The film was funny and surprisingly profound as it explored belonging, grief and the bond between animals and humans. It made me think of “Bambi”, “Up”, “Coco” and other children’s films that grapple with the heavy theme of loss.  Little wonder, then, that “The Sheep Detectives” makes it onto our list of the best films and TV shows of the year so far. (We appreciate, too, that busy people are short on screen time, so we’ve listed a number of titles you can safely skip.) There are suggestions for every mood and whim, from sitcoms and satires to biopics, documentaries and literary adaptations. These are the titles, you might say, that streams are made of. Rachel Lloyd, Deputy culture editor Editor’s picks The best, and worst, TV series and films of 2026 (so far) Our suggestions of what to watch, and what to avoid, on your summer holiday Why you should never skip a TV intro Title sequences shape the way you watch television. They are dwindling What “Backrooms” and “Obsession” reveal about Gen Z’s fears Two new horror films have hit a nerve with youngsters Steven Spielberg has more to say about aliens Returning to them in “Disclosure Day”, the great director raises the stakes Why the sex in “Rivals” is more than mere titillation In the hit TV drama, back for a second season, a bonk is never just a bonk Go beyond the headlines Get unlimited digital access to The Economist by subscribing today.
Продовжити читати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/best-film-television-2026-so-far-the-economist-qkwme?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BCz2oEs8aTnC6HKpX3s9DgQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=21BnbtOkgQgYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-fz96fz~mqrz9r1c~1y-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqrz9r1c-1y

Fear and Greed is at
20
(Fear)
and BTC is near
$62K
this morning. Bitcoin-led selling and derivatives liquidations
have kept the market under pressure, with the SpaceX IPO drawing
capital away from crypto and six consecutive weeks of ETF
outflows removing $6.35 billion from Bitcoin products. The PCE
inflation print drops later today, and consensus expects a
reading that keeps rate hike fears alive

Two of crypto's most watched organisations made major structural
moves yesterday. One is cutting deeply. One is expanding into a
space worth trillions. Both decisions will shape where the next
wave of crypto capital goes.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Meta Is Building a Prediction Market App Called Arena

Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has told employees to build a
prediction market app called Arena, according to the New York
Times. The product covers politics, sports, entertainment, and
world affairs, and is described as a top priority inside the
company.

Real money betting has not been ruled out.

What happened to Meta's previous prediction market app and when
did it shut down?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/GpF-I44Rvv29vttqCASIKw~~/AAQRxRA~/jAMfOk4vlwql0nlzkcTNfS56i14Wvos7Y4t45LQzUrWsJiSCCYP2Iom2iPlkcH3YLlfpAC3x-6ZWv8Y3Atr0mVsfgJV40D7VTC0qTnrQRze-jtxrMcPM3xsnJodbmOLYKVta8sUD759YQXH7tY3nnmjQ956QTlnb-AL-eaNOGlrHI7ucQbGa37_vNfvS7M8icbOWm7JlWCBQXVBIfHYF5-Lhe_heT
